rule: Trending Audio = Algorithmic Boost — Videos using trending audio receive 2.4x more impressions on TikTok. The algorithm actively pushes content using sounds with velocity (rapid saves/uses). Peak window is 48-72h after a sound starts trending.

Audio Hook in First 3 Seconds — The audio drop, beat change, or voiceover reveal in the first 3 seconds creates a retention spike. Videos that delay audio hook past 3s lose 31% of viewers before it lands.

85% Watch Without Sound — Optimize for Both — 85% of Facebook and 40% of TikTok views happen without audio. Content must work visually with captions while also rewarding audio-on viewers with extra value (music, voice energy).

Original Audio Builds Creator Authority — Creators who produce original sounds that others use gain compound reach. Original audio saves correlate 0.73 with follower conversion rate. Encourage unique voiceover styles over generic music.

Music BPM Must Match Edit Pacing — Cut frequency should align with music tempo. 120-140 BPM for fast cuts (2-3s/cut), 80-100 BPM for medium cuts (4-6s/cut). Mismatched BPM/cut rate reduces watch time by 18%.

Voice Energy is Retention Signal — Monotone voiceover loses 44% of viewers by second 15. Dynamic voice (pitch variation, strategic pauses, emphasis on key words) keeps completion rate 2.1x higher. Sentence-end drops lose attention.

KR/SEA: Music-First Content Strategy — Korea and Southeast Asia audiences engage with music-driven content 3.2x more than talk-first. Music sets emotional context before hook. Lyrics and music reactions outperform pure commentary.

Loopable Audio Drives Replay Rate — Audio that loops seamlessly (no abrupt end) increases TikTok replay rate by 34%. Continuous music without a definitive ending tricks the autoplay loop. Replay rate >15% = major distribution signal.
